required_units.meaning: Micro-teaching-unit IDs required by an exercise or exam question.

required_skills.meaning: Legacy/source field currently used in external and authored source data; do not reuse for new skill-tag or operation semantics.

exercise_operations.meaning: Fine-grained learner actions inside an exercise.

skill_tags.meaning: Broader taxonomy labels; CP-4 must decide vocabulary coexistence and migration rules.

coexistence_issues.summary: Dry-run overlays use English-style skill_tags while skill-categories.md uses Dutch broad categories.
coexistence_issues.next_action: CP-4 should decide whether to normalize overlay tags to Dutch broad categories, keep English sub-tags as aliases, or split broad tags from operation labels.
coexistence_issues.proof_required_to_close: Registry records show approved tag IDs, alias policy, and validator rules.

coexistence_issues.summary: exercise_operations are useful but not yet governed machine registry records.
coexistence_issues.next_action: Keep operations provisional until CP-4 approves governance and a later CLI-backed registry path exists.
coexistence_issues.proof_required_to_close: Operation records have stable IDs, statuses, source provenance, validator coverage, and promotion policy.

coexistence_issues.summary: required_skills remains a legacy/source field and must not be reused for a new concept.
coexistence_issues.next_action: Validators and review packet should preserve required_units/exercise_operations/skill_tags separation.
coexistence_issues.proof_required_to_close: CP-4 closure confirms field semantics and blocks required_skills reuse until migration.
